label: Public-source evidence fixture; no partnership or licensing claims

records.title: Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport sustainability environment
records.body: The Metropolitan Airports Commission owns and operates MSP and has adopted sustainability goals covering emissions, water, materials and employee engagement, including a goal to reduce, reuse, recycle or compost 75% of solid waste by 2030.
records.publisher: Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport / Metropolitan Airports Commission

records.title: MSP concession organics separation and off-site composting program
records.body: Since 2009, MSP has required food and beverage concessionaires to separate compostable organic material from back-of-house recycling and trash streams; the material is collected and sent off-site for composting.

records.title: MSP diverted more than 500 tons of organics in 2025
records.body: MSP reported diverting more than 500 tons of organics for compost in 2025 and using Oscar AI units to help passengers sort waste, demonstrating both material throughput and continuing demand for improved sorting, measurement and diversion performance.

records.title: Oscar AI computer-vision waste sorting assistant
records.body: Oscar uses computer vision to identify waste items and direct users to recycling, compost or landfill bins. MSP has publicly reported piloting and deploying Oscar AI sorting units in its terminals.

records.title: US9475698B2 — Hydrothermal carbonization of sewage wastes
records.body: The University of Minnesota patent describes hydrothermal treatment of wet wastes to obtain products including hydrochar fuels, fatty acids, recyclable metals and phosphorus products. The patent is evidence of a wet-waste conversion capability; applicability to MSP food waste requires technical validation and licensing review.

records.title: University of Minnesota hydrothermal carbonization research on wet organic feedstocks
records.body: University of Minnesota researchers investigated hydrothermal carbonization of wet distiller's grains and reported production of char with high heat content, establishing research capability relevant to wet organic feedstock conversion. Airport-food-waste applicability remains an inference requiring testing.

records.title: FAA airport recycling, reuse and waste-reduction planning requirements
records.body: FAA guidance states that federally obligated airports preparing or updating master plans must address recycling feasibility, waste minimization, operations and maintenance, waste contracts, and potential cost savings or revenue. This is a planning and policy pathway, not a confirmed project grant.

records.title: EPA avoided-landfilled-food-waste methane methodology and calculator
records.body: EPA provides a peer-reviewed methodology and calculator for estimating annual avoided methane emissions per ton of food waste diverted from landfill. It can support baseline and diversion claims but does not by itself establish durable carbon-removal credits.

records.title: MSP concession and food-service operators participating in food recovery
records.body: MSP identified Aero Service Group, Areas USA, Estes, Hudson, Stellar Partners, OTG, HMSHost, Delaware North, Caribou Coffee and SSP America as participants or early participants in airport food-donation activity. These entities are potential operational stakeholders, not confirmed buyers or project partners for this opportunity.

records.title: EPA food-waste pathway hierarchy and life-cycle evidence
records.body: EPA reports that food waste is a major component of landfilled municipal waste and evaluates multiple management pathways using life-cycle and circularity methods. Any MSP opportunity must distinguish prevention, donation, composting, energy recovery, conversion and landfill-diversion claims rather than treating them as equivalent carbon outcomes.
